mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals expanding output capacity and economic growth, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows is negative or forward returns in top decile of INDPRO growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops sharply below 4%, the labor market overheats, triggering Fed tightening expectations and wage inflation, which compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns in triggered instances are positive in more than 60% of cases, or Fed funds rate does not rise within 3 months after trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate combined with accelerating consumer price inflation signals aggressive monetary tightening to combat inflation, which tightens financial conditions and reduces equity risk appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In a linear regression of forward 20-day return on FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL, the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in retail sales (RSAFS year-over-year growth above 5%) indicates strong consumer demand, driving revenue growth and positive earnings surprise, which lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next month during recovery phases.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are significantly negative (mean < 0) in triggered periods, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline over any 3-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse of housing starts below 1,500 thousand units signals a severe contraction in residential investment, a leading indicator of recession, which depresses corporate profits and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day return is positive in more than 40% of triggered events, or the count of triggered events is fewer than 10 over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with sluggish payroll employment growth indicates strong productivity gains as output expands without commensurate hiring, boosting profit margins and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal (INDPRO rise + PAYEMS stagnation) does not produce a mean forward 60-day return greater than the unconditional mean, or the interaction term in a regression is not positive and significant at 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: A higher federal funds rate increases the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lowering present equity values and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between changes in FEDFUNDS and forward 5-day returns is positive over any 2-year rolling window, or the mean return is not significantly negative in high-rate periods (FEDFUNDS > 2%).

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation regime (CPI year-over-year below 2%), a moderate rise in inflation reflects healthy demand growth without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, which supports consumer spending and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In months where CPI YoY is below 2% and CPIAUCSL rose above its 12-month average, the mean forward 20-day S&P 500 return is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10) or is negative.
